briefdescription.para: Use the Timsort algorithm provided in timsort.h to sort nodeset as this is a great improvement over the old Shell sort used in xmlXPathNodeSetSort.
Macro to raise an XPath error and return NULL.

memberdef.name: XP_TEST_HIT
memberdef.initializer: if (hasAxisRange != 0) { \ if (++pos == maxPos) { \ if (addNode(seq, cur) < 0) \ xmlXPathPErrMemory(ctxt); \ goto axis_range_end; } \ } else { \ if (addNode(seq, cur) < 0) \ xmlXPathPErrMemory(ctxt); \ if (breakOnFirstHit) goto first_hit; }

memberdef.name: XP_TEST_HIT_NS
memberdef.initializer: if (hasAxisRange != 0) { \ if (++pos == maxPos) { \ hasNsNodes = 1; \ if (xmlXPathNodeSetAddNs(seq, xpctxt->node, (xmlNsPtr) cur) < 0) \ xmlXPathPErrMemory(ctxt); \ goto axis_range_end; } \ } else { \ hasNsNodes = 1; \ if (xmlXPathNodeSetAddNs(seq, xpctxt->node, (xmlNsPtr) cur) < 0) \ xmlXPathPErrMemory(ctxt); \ if (breakOnFirstHit) goto first_hit; }

memberdef.name: xmlXPathStandardFunctions
memberdef.initializer: = { { "boolean", xmlXPathBooleanFunction }, { "ceiling", xmlXPathCeilingFunction }, { "count", xmlXPathCountFunction }, { "concat", xmlXPathConcatFunction }, { "contains", xmlXPathContainsFunction }, { "id", xmlXPathIdFunction }, { "false", xmlXPathFalseFunction }, { "floor", xmlXPathFloorFunction }, { "last", xmlXPathLastFunction }, { "lang", xmlXPathLangFunction }, { "local-name", xmlXPathLocalNameFunction }, { "not", xmlXPathNotFunction }, { "name", xmlXPathNameFunction }, { "namespace-uri", xmlXPathNamespaceURIFunction }, { "normalize-space", xmlXPathNormalizeFunction }, { "number", xmlXPathNumberFunction }, { "position", xmlXPathPositionFunction }, { "round", xmlXPathRoundFunction }, { "string", xmlXPathStringFunction }, { "string-length", xmlXPathStringLengthFunction }, { "starts-with", xmlXPathStartsWithFunction }, { "substring", xmlXPathSubstringFunction }, { "substring-before", xmlXPathSubstringBeforeFunction }, { "substring-after", xmlXPathSubstringAfterFunction }, { "sum", xmlXPathSumFunction }, { "true", xmlXPathTrueFunction }, { "translate", xmlXPathTranslateFunction } }

memberdef.name: xmlXPathErrorMessages
memberdef.initializer: = { "Ok", "Number encoding", "Unfinished literal", "Start of literal", "Expected $ for variable reference", "Undefined variable", "Invalid predicate", "Invalid expression", "Missing closing curly brace", "Unregistered function", "Invalid operand", "Invalid type", "Invalid number of arguments", "Invalid context size", "Invalid context position", "Memory allocation error", "Syntax error", "Resource error", "Sub resource error", "Undefined namespace prefix", "Encoding error", "Char out of XML range", "Invalid or incomplete context", "Stack usage error", "Forbidden variable", "Operation limit exceeded", "Recursion limit exceeded", "?? Unknown error ??" }

briefdescription.para: Implement the name() XPath function string name(node-set?) The name function returns a string containing a QName representing the name of the node in the argument node-set that is first in document order.

detaileddescription.para: The QName must represent the name with respect to the namespace declarations in effect on the node whose name is being represented. Typically, this will be the form in which the name occurred in the XML source. This need not be the case if there are namespace declarations in effect on the node that associate multiple prefixes with the same namespace. However, an implementation may include information about the original prefix in its representation of nodes; in this case, an implementation can ensure that the returned string is always the same as the QName used in the XML source. If the argument it omitted it defaults to the context node. Libxml keep the original prefix so the "real qualified name" used is returned.

briefdescription.para: Initialize the XPath environment.
Checks whether a double is a NaN.

simplesect.para: 1 if the value is a NaN, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Checks whether a double is an infinity.

simplesect.para: 1 if the value is +Infinite, -1 if -Infinite, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Clears the list from all temporary XPath objects (e.g.

detaileddescription.para: namespace nodes are feed), but does not free the list itself. Sets the length of the list to 0.

parameterdescription.para: the node set might contain namespace nodes

briefdescription.para: Compare two nodes w.r.t document order.

detaileddescription.para: This one is optimized for handling of non-element nodes.

simplesect.para: -2 in case of error 1 if first point < second point, 0 if it's the same node, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Comparison function for the Timsort implementation.

simplesect.para: -2 in case of error -1 if first point < second point, 0 if it's the same node, +1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Handle a memory allocation failure.
Handle a memory allocation failure.
Handle an XPath error.
Handle an XPath error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContext *ctxt, const char *file, int line, int no)
memberdef.name: xmlXPatherror

briefdescription.para: Formats an error message.
Adds opCount to the running total of operations and returns -1 if the operation limit is exceeded.

detaileddescription.para: Returns 0 otherwise.

parameterdescription.para: the number of operations to be added

briefdescription.para: Depending on the state of the cache this frees the given XPath object or stores it in the cache.

parameterdescription.para: the xmlXPathObject to free or to cache

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the Precompiled XPath operation searching only the first element in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the XPath parser context with the compiled expression
the first elem found so far

simplesect.para: the number of examined objects.

briefdescription.para: Evaluates if the expression evaluates to true.

simplesect.para: 1 if true, 0 if false and -1 on API or internal errors.

briefdescription.para: Create a new Xpath component.

simplesect.para: the newly allocated xmlXPathCompExpr or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Free up the memory allocated by comp

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, int ch1, int ch2, xmlXPathOp op, int value, int value2, int value3, void *value4, void *value5)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathCompExprAdd

briefdescription.para: Add a step to an XPath Compiled Expression.

simplesect.para: -1 in case of failure, the index otherwise

briefdescription.para: Dump the content of the object for debugging purposes.

parameterdescription.para: the FILE * to dump the output

memberdef.argsstring: (FILE *output, xmlXPathCompExprPtr comp, xmlXPathStepOpPtr op, int depth)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathDebugDumpStepOp

briefdescription.para: Dumps the tree of the compiled XPath expression.
Create a new object cache.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathCache just allocated.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathContext *ctxt, int active, int value, int options)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathContextSetCache

briefdescription.para: Creates/frees an object cache on the XPath context.

detaileddescription.para: If activates XPath objects (xmlXPathObject) will be cached internally to be reused.
options must be set to 0 to enable XPath object caching. Other values for options have currently no effect.
value sets the maximum number of XPath objects to be cached per slot. There are two slots for node-set and misc objects. Use <0 for the default number (100).

parameterdescription.para: a value with semantics dependent on options
options (currently only the value 0 is used)

simplesect.para: 0 if the setting succeeded, and -1 on API or internal errors.

briefdescription.para: This is the cached version of xmlXPathWrapNodeSet.

detaileddescription.para: Wrap the Nodeset val in a new xmlXPathObject
In case of error the node set is destroyed and NULL is returned.

simplesect.para: the created or reused object.

briefdescription.para: This is the cached version of xmlXPathWrapString.

detaileddescription.para: Wraps the val string into an XPath object.

simplesect.para: the created or reused object.

briefdescription.para: This is the cached version of xmlXPathNewNodeSet.

detaileddescription.para: Acquire an xmlXPathObject of type NodeSet and initialize it with the single Node val

simplesect.para: the created or reused object.

briefdescription.para: This is the cached version of xmlXPathNewString.

detaileddescription.para: Acquire an xmlXPathObject of type string and of value val

simplesect.para: the created or reused object.

briefdescription.para: This is the cached version of xmlXPathNewCString.

detaileddescription.para: Acquire an xmlXPathObject of type string and of value val

simplesect.para: the created or reused object.

briefdescription.para: This is the cached version of xmlXPathNewBoolean.

detaileddescription.para: Acquires an xmlXPathObject of type boolean and of value val

simplesect.para: the created or reused object.

briefdescription.para: This is the cached version of xmlXPathNewFloat.

detaileddescription.para: Acquires an xmlXPathObject of type double and of value val

simplesect.para: the created or reused object.

briefdescription.para: This is the cached version of xmlXPathObjectCopy.

detaileddescription.para: Acquire a copy of a given object

simplesect.para: a created or reused created object.

briefdescription.para: Converts an XPath object to its number value.

simplesect.para: the number value

briefdescription.para: Pops the top XPath object from the value stack.

simplesect.para: the XPath object just removed

briefdescription.para: Pushes a new XPath object on top of the value stack.

detaileddescription.para: If value is NULL, a memory error is recorded in the parser context.
The object is destroyed in case of error.

simplesect.para: the number of items on the value stack, or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Pops a boolean from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the boolean

briefdescription.para: Pops a number from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the number

briefdescription.para: Pops a string from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the string

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the node-set

briefdescription.para: Pops an external object from the stack, handling conversion if needed.

detaileddescription.para: Check error with xmlXPathCheckError.

simplesect.para: the object

briefdescription.para: Convert the number into a string representation.
Call this routine to speed up XPath computation on static documents.

detaileddescription.para: Evaluation functions.

This stamps all the element nodes with the document order Like for line information, the order is kept in the element->content field, the value stored is actually - the node number (starting at -1) to be able to differentiate from line numbers.

simplesect.para: the number of elements found in the document or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Compare two nodes w.r.t document order.

simplesect.para: -2 in case of error 1 if first point < second point, 0 if it's the same node, -1 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Sort the node set in document order.
Namespace node in libxml don't match the XPath semantic.

detaileddescription.para: In a node set the namespace nodes are duplicated and the next pointer is set to the parent node in the XPath semantic.

parameterdescription.para: the parent node of the namespace XPath node

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Namespace nodes in libxml don't match the XPath semantic.

detaileddescription.para: Really internal functions.
In a node set the namespace nodes are duplicated and the next pointer is set to the parent node in the XPath semantic. Check if such a node needs to be freed

parameterdescription.para: the XPath namespace node found in a nodeset.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlNodeSet of type double and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: checks whether cur contains val

detaileddescription.para: NodeSet handling.

simplesect.para: true (1) if cur contains val, false (0) otherwise

briefdescription.para: add a new namespace node to an existing NodeSet

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: add a new xmlNode to an existing NodeSet

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: add a new xmlNode to an existing NodeSet, optimized version when we are sure the node is not already in the set.

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success and -1 in case of failure

briefdescription.para: Merges two nodesets, all nodes from val2 are added to val1 if val1 is NULL, a new set is created and copied from val2

detaileddescription.para: Frees val1 in case of error.

simplesect.para: val1 once extended or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Merges two nodesets, all nodes from set2 are added to set1.

detaileddescription.para: Checks for duplicate nodes. Clears set2.
Frees set1 in case of error.

simplesect.para: set1 once extended or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Merges two nodesets, all nodes from set2 are added to set1.

detaileddescription.para: Doesn't check for duplicate nodes. Clears set2.
Frees set1 in case of error.

simplesect.para: set1 once extended or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Removes an xmlNode from an existing NodeSet.
Removes an entry from an existing NodeSet list.
Free the NodeSet compound (not the actual nodes !).
Clears the list from temporary XPath objects (e.g.

detaileddescription.para: namespace nodes are feed) starting with the entry at pos, but does not free the list itself. Sets the length of the list to pos.

parameterdescription.para: the node set to be cleared
the start position to clear from
the node set might contain namespace nodes

briefdescription.para: Move the last node to the first position and clear temporary XPath objects (e.g.

detaileddescription.para: namespace nodes) from all other nodes. Sets the length of the list to 1.

parameterdescription.para: the node set to be cleared

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type NodeSet and initialize it with the single Node val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type Value Tree (XSLT) and initialize it with the tree root val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type NodeSet and initialize it with the Nodeset val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Wrap the Nodeset val in a new xmlXPathObject.

detaileddescription.para: In case of error the node set is destroyed and NULL is returned.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Free up the xmlXPathObject obj but don't deallocate the objects in the list contrary to xmlXPathFreeObject.
Implements the EXSLT - Sets difference() function: node-set set:difference (node-set, node-set)

simplesect.para: the difference between the two node sets, or nodes1 if nodes2 is empty

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets intersection() function: node-set set:intersection (node-set, node-set)

simplesect.para: a node set comprising the nodes that are within both the node sets passed as arguments

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets distinct() function: node-set set:distinct (node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: a subset of the nodes contained in nodes, or nodes if it is empty

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets distinct() function: node-set set:distinct (node-set) nodes is sorted by document order, then exslSetsDistinctSorted is called with the sorted node-set.

simplesect.para: a subset of the nodes contained in nodes, or nodes if it is empty

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets has-same-nodes function: boolean set:has-same-node(node-set, node-set)

simplesect.para: true (1) if nodes1 shares any node with nodes2, false (0) otherwise

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets leading() function: node-set set:leading (node-set, node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes that precede node in document order, nodes if node is NULL or an empty node-set if nodes doesn't contain node

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets leading() function: node-set set:leading (node-set, node-set) nodes is sorted by document order, then exslSetsNodeLeadingSorted is called.

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes that precede node in document order, nodes if node is NULL or an empty node-set if nodes doesn't contain node

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets leading() function: node-set set:leading (node-set, node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order
a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes1 that precede the first node in nodes2 in document order, nodes1 if nodes2 is NULL or empty or an empty node-set if nodes1 doesn't contain nodes2

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets leading() function: node-set set:leading (node-set, node-set) nodes1 and nodes2 are sorted by document order, then exslSetsLeadingSorted is called.

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes1 that precede the first node in nodes2 in document order, nodes1 if nodes2 is NULL or empty or an empty node-set if nodes1 doesn't contain nodes2

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets trailing() function: node-set set:trailing (node-set, node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes that follow node in document order, nodes if node is NULL or an empty node-set if nodes doesn't contain node

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets trailing() function: node-set set:trailing (node-set, node-set) nodes is sorted by document order, then xmlXPathNodeTrailingSorted is called.

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes that follow node in document order, nodes if node is NULL or an empty node-set if nodes doesn't contain node

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets trailing() function: node-set set:trailing (node-set, node-set)

parameterdescription.para: a node-set, sorted by document order
a node-set, sorted by document order

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes1 that follow the first node in nodes2 in document order, nodes1 if nodes2 is NULL or empty or an empty node-set if nodes1 doesn't contain nodes2

briefdescription.para: Implements the EXSLT - Sets trailing() function: node-set set:trailing (node-set, node-set) nodes1 and nodes2 are sorted by document order, then xmlXPathTrailingSorted is called.

simplesect.para: the nodes in nodes1 that follow the first node in nodes2 in document order, nodes1 if nodes2 is NULL or empty or an empty node-set if nodes1 doesn't contain nodes2

briefdescription.para: Register a new function.

detaileddescription.para: If f is NULL it unregisters the function

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Search in the Variable array of the context for the given variable value.

simplesect.para: the a copy of the value or NULL if not found

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the XPath context data associated to registered variables.

If ns_uri is NULL it unregisters the namespace

parameterdescription.para: the namespace prefix cannot be NULL or empty string

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success, -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Search in the namespace declaration array of the context for the given namespace name associated to the given prefix.

simplesect.para: the value or NULL if not found

briefdescription.para: Cleanup the XPath context data associated to registered variables.
Create a new xmlXPathObject of type double and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type boolean and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathObject of type string and of value val

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

detaileddescription.para: Frees val in case of error.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

simplesect.para: the newly created object.

detaileddescription.para: Objects and Nodesets handling.

briefdescription.para: Converts a boolean to its string value.

simplesect.para: a newly allocated string.

briefdescription.para: Converts a number to its string value.

simplesect.para: a newly allocated string.

briefdescription.para: Converts a node to its string value.

simplesect.para: a newly allocated string.

briefdescription.para: Converts a node-set to its string value.

simplesect.para: a newly allocated string.

briefdescription.para: Converts an existing object to its string() equivalent.

simplesect.para: the allocated string value of the object, NULL in case of error. It's up to the caller to free the string memory with xmlFree.

briefdescription.para: Converts an existing object to its string() equivalent.

simplesect.para: the number value

briefdescription.para: Converts an XPath object to its number value.

briefdescription.para: Converts an existing object to its number() equivalent.

simplesect.para: the new object, the old one is freed (or the operation is done directly on val)

simplesect.para: the boolean value

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathContext.

detaileddescription.para: Context handling.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathContext just allocated. The caller will need to free it.

briefdescription.para: Free up an xmlXPathContext.
Register a callback function that will be called on errors and warnings.

detaileddescription.para: If handler is NULL, the error handler will be deactivated.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathParserContext.

detaileddescription.para: Utilities to extend XPath.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathParserContext just allocated.

briefdescription.para: Create a new xmlXPathParserContext when processing a compiled expression.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathParserContext just allocated.

briefdescription.para: Free up an xmlXPathParserContext.
Function computing the beginning of the string value of the node, used to speed up comparisons.

simplesect.para: an int usable as a hash

briefdescription.para: Function computing the beginning of the string value of the node, used to speed up comparisons.

simplesect.para: an int usable as a hash

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, int inf, int strict, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg, xmlXPathObjectPtr f)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathCompareNodeSetFloat

briefdescription.para: Implement the compare operation between a nodeset and a number ns < val (1, 1, ... ns <= val (1, 0, ... ns > val (0, 1, ... ns >= val (0, 0, ...

detaileddescription.para: If one object to be compared is a node-set and the other is a number, then the comparison will be true if and only if there is a node in the node-set such that the result of performing the comparison on the number to be compared and on the result of converting the string-value of that node to a number using the number function is true.

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, int inf, int strict, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg, xmlXPathObjectPtr s)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathCompareNodeSetString

briefdescription.para: Implement the compare operation between a nodeset and a string ns < val (1, 1, ... ns <= val (1, 0, ... ns > val (0, 1, ... ns >= val (0, 0, ...

detaileddescription.para: If one object to be compared is a node-set and the other is a string, then the comparison will be true if and only if there is a node in the node-set such that the result of performing the comparison on the string-value of the node and the other string is true.

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, int inf, int strict, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg1, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg2)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathCompareNodeSets

briefdescription.para: Implement the compare operation on nodesets:

detaileddescription.para:
If both objects to be compared are node-sets, then the comparison will be true if and only if there is a node in the first node-set and a node in the second node-set such that the result of performing the comparison on the string-values of the two nodes is true. .... When neither object to be compared is a node-set and the operator is <=, <, >= or >, then the objects are compared by converting both objects to numbers and comparing the numbers according to IEEE 754. .... The number function converts its argument to a number as follows:
a string that consists of optional whitespace followed by an optional minus sign followed by a Number followed by whitespace is converted to the IEEE 754 number that is nearest (according to the IEEE 754 round-to-nearest rule) to the mathematical value represented by the string; any other string is converted to NaN
Conclusion all nodes need to be converted first to their string value and then the comparison must be done when possible

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, int inf, int strict, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg, xmlXPathObjectPtr val)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathCompareNodeSetValue

briefdescription.para: Implement the compare operation between a nodeset and a value ns < val (1, 1, ... ns <= val (1, 0, ... ns > val (0, 1, ... ns >= val (0, 0, ...

detaileddescription.para: If one object to be compared is a node-set and the other is a boolean, then the comparison will be true if and only if the result of performing the comparison on the boolean and on the result of converting the node-set to a boolean using the boolean function is true.

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg, const xmlChar *str, int neq)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathEqualNodeSetString

briefdescription.para: Implement the equal operation on XPath objects content: arg1 == arg2 If one object to be compared is a node-set and the other is a string, then the comparison will be true if and only if there is a node in the node-set such that the result of performing the comparison on the string-value of the node and the other string is true.

parameterdescription.para: flag to show whether for '=' (0) or '!=' (1)

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg, double f, int neq)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathEqualNodeSetFloat

briefdescription.para: Implement the equal operation on XPath objects content: arg1 == arg2 If one object to be compared is a node-set and the other is a number, then the comparison will be true if and only if there is a node in the node-set such that the result of performing the comparison on the number to be compared and on the result of converting the string-value of that node to a number using the number function is true.

parameterdescription.para: flag to show whether to compare '=' (0) or '!=' (1)

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg1, xmlXPathObjectPtr arg2, int neq)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathEqualNodeSets

briefdescription.para: Implement the equal / not equal operation on XPath nodesets: arg1 == arg2 or arg1 != arg2 If both objects to be compared are node-sets, then the comparison will be true if and only if there is a node in the first node-set and a node in the second node-set such that the result of performing the comparison on the string-values of the two nodes is true.

detaileddescription.para: (needless to say, this is a costly operation)

parameterdescription.para: flag to show whether to test '=' (0) or '!=' (1)

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

briefdescription.para: Implement the equal operation on XPath objects content: arg1 == arg2

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

briefdescription.para: Implement the equal operation on XPath objects content: arg1 == arg2

simplesect.para: 0 or 1 depending on the results of the test.

briefdescription.para: Implement the compare operation on XPath objects: arg1 < arg2 (1, 1, ... arg1 <= arg2 (1, 0, ... arg1 > arg2 (0, 1, ... arg1 >= arg2 (0, 0, ...

detaileddescription.para: When neither object to be compared is a node-set and the operator is <=, <, >=, >, then the objects are compared by converted both objects to numbers and comparing the numbers according to IEEE 754. The < comparison will be true if and only if the first number is less than the second number. The <= comparison will be true if and only if the first number is less than or equal to the second number. The > comparison will be true if and only if the first number is greater than the second number. The >= comparison will be true if and only if the first number is greater than or equal to the second number.

simplesect.para: 1 if the comparison succeeded, 0 if it failed

briefdescription.para: Implement the unary - operation on an XPath object The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Implement the add operation on XPath objects: The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Implement the subtraction operation on XPath objects: The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Implement the multiply operation on XPath objects: The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Implement the div operation on XPath objects arg1 / arg2.

detaileddescription.para: The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.

briefdescription.para: Implement the mod operation on XPath objects: arg1 / arg2 The numeric operators convert their operands to numbers as if by calling the number function.
Traversal function for the "self" direction The self axis contains just the context node itself.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "child" direction The child axis contains the children of the context node in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "child" direction and nodes of type element.

detaileddescription.para: The child axis contains the children of the context node in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "descendant" direction the descendant axis contains the descendants of the context node in document order; a descendant is a child or a child of a child and so on.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "descendant-or-self" direction the descendant-or-self axis contains the context node and the descendants of the context node in document order; thus the context node is the first node on the axis, and the first child of the context node is the second node on the axis.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "parent" direction The parent axis contains the parent of the context node, if there is one.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "ancestor" direction the ancestor axis contains the ancestors of the context node; the ancestors of the context node consist of the parent of context node and the parent's parent and so on; the nodes are ordered in reverse document order; thus the parent is the first node on the axis, and the parent's parent is the second node on the axis.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "ancestor-or-self" direction he ancestor-or-self axis contains the context node and ancestors of the context node in reverse document order; thus the context node is the first node on the axis, and the context node's parent the second; parent here is defined the same as with the parent axis.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "following-sibling" direction The following-sibling axis contains the following siblings of the context node in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "preceding-sibling" direction The preceding-sibling axis contains the preceding siblings of the context node in reverse document order; the first preceding sibling is first on the axis; the sibling preceding that node is the second on the axis and so on.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "following" direction The following axis contains all nodes in the same document as the context node that are after the context node in document order, excluding any descendants and excluding attribute nodes and namespace nodes; the nodes are ordered in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "preceding" direction the preceding axis contains all nodes in the same document as the context node that are before the context node in document order, excluding any ancestors and excluding attribute nodes and namespace nodes; the nodes are ordered in reverse document order.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "preceding" direction the preceding axis contains all nodes in the same document as the context node that are before the context node in document order, excluding any ancestors and excluding attribute nodes and namespace nodes; the nodes are ordered in reverse document order This is a faster implementation but internal only since it requires a state kept in the parser context: ctxt->ancestor.

parameterdescription.para: the current node in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "namespace" direction the namespace axis contains the namespace nodes of the context node; the order of nodes on this axis is implementation-defined; the axis will be empty unless the context node is an element.

detaileddescription.para: We keep the XML namespace node at the end of the list.

parameterdescription.para: the current attribute in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Traversal function for the "attribute" direction TODO: support DTD inherited default attributes.

parameterdescription.para: the current attribute in the traversal

simplesect.para: the next element following that axis

briefdescription.para: Initialize the context to the root of the document.
Implement the last() XPath function number last() The last function returns the number of nodes in the context node list.
Implement the position() XPath function number position() The position function returns the position of the context node in the context node list.

detaileddescription.para: The first position is 1, and so the last position will be equal to last().

briefdescription.para: Implement the count() XPath function number count(node-set)
Selects elements by their unique ID.

parameterdescription.para: a whitespace separated list of IDs

simplesect.para: a node-set of selected elements.

briefdescription.para: Implement the id() XPath function node-set id(object) The id function selects elements by their unique ID (see [5.2.1 Unique IDs]).

detaileddescription.para: When the argument to id is of type node-set, then the result is the union of the result of applying id to the string value of each of the nodes in the argument node-set. When the argument to id is of any other type, the argument is converted to a string as if by a call to the string function; the string is split into a whitespace-separated list of tokens (whitespace is any sequence of characters matching the production S); the result is a node-set containing the elements in the same document as the context node that have a unique ID equal to any of the tokens in the list.

briefdescription.para: Implement the local-name() XPath function string local-name(node-set?) The local-name function returns a string containing the local part of the name of the node in the argument node-set that is first in document order.

detaileddescription.para: If the node-set is empty or the first node has no name, an empty string is returned. If the argument is omitted it defaults to the context node.

briefdescription.para: Implement the namespace-uri() XPath function string namespace-uri(node-set?) The namespace-uri function returns a string containing the namespace URI of the expanded name of the node in the argument node-set that is first in document order.

detaileddescription.para: If the node-set is empty, the first node has no name, or the expanded name has no namespace URI, an empty string is returned. If the argument is omitted it defaults to the context node.

briefdescription.para: Implement the string() XPath function string string(object?) The string function converts an object to a string as follows:

listitem.para: A node-set is converted to a string by returning the value of the node in the node-set that is first in document order. If the node-set is empty, an empty string is returned.

A number is converted to a string as follows
NaN is converted to the string NaN
positive zero is converted to the string 0
negative zero is converted to the string 0
positive infinity is converted to the string Infinity
negative infinity is converted to the string -Infinity
if the number is an integer, the number is represented in decimal form as a Number with no decimal point and no leading zeros, preceded by a minus sign (-) if the number is negative
otherwise, the number is represented in decimal form as a Number including a decimal point with at least one digit before the decimal point and at least one digit after the decimal point, preceded by a minus sign (-) if the number is negative; there must be no leading zeros before the decimal point apart possibly from the one required digit immediately before the decimal point; beyond the one required digit after the decimal point there must be as many, but only as many, more digits as are needed to uniquely distinguish the number from all other IEEE 754 numeric values.
The boolean false value is converted to the string false. The boolean true value is converted to the string true.

detaileddescription.para: If the argument is omitted, it defaults to a node-set with the context node as its only member.

briefdescription.para: Implement the string-length() XPath function number string-length(string?) The string-length returns the number of characters in the string (see [3.6 Strings]).

detaileddescription.para: If the argument is omitted, it defaults to the context node converted to a string, in other words the value of the context node.

briefdescription.para: Implement the concat() XPath function string concat(string, string, string*) The concat function returns the concatenation of its arguments.

Implement the contains() XPath function boolean contains(string, string) The contains function returns true if the first argument string contains the second argument string, and otherwise returns false.

Implement the starts-with() XPath function boolean starts-with(string, string) The starts-with function returns true if the first argument string starts with the second argument string, and otherwise returns false.

Implement the substring() XPath function string substring(string, number, number?) The substring function returns the substring of the first argument starting at the position specified in the second argument with length specified in the third argument.

detaileddescription.para:
For example, substring("12345",2,3) returns "234". If the third argument is not specified, it returns the substring starting at the position specified in the second argument and continuing to the end of the string. For example, substring("12345",2) returns "2345". More precisely, each character in the string (see [3.6 Strings]) is considered to have a numeric position: the position of the first character is 1, the position of the second character is 2 and so on. The returned substring contains those characters for which the position of the character is greater than or equal to the second argument and, if the third argument is specified, less than the sum of the second and third arguments; the comparisons and addition used for the above follow the standard IEEE 754 rules. Thus:
substring("12345", 1.5, 2.6) returns "234"
substring("12345", 0, 3) returns "12"
substring("12345", 0 div 0, 3) returns ""
substring("12345", 1, 0 div 0) returns ""
substring("12345", -42, 1 div 0) returns "12345"
substring("12345", -1 div 0, 1 div 0) returns ""

briefdescription.para: Implement the substring-before() XPath function string substring-before(string, string) The substring-before function returns the substring of the first argument string that precedes the first occurrence of the second argument string in the first argument string, or the empty string if the first argument string does not contain the second argument string.

detaileddescription.para: For example, substring-before("1999/04/01","/") returns 1999.

briefdescription.para: Implement the substring-after() XPath function string substring-after(string, string) The substring-after function returns the substring of the first argument string that follows the first occurrence of the second argument string in the first argument string, or the empty string if the first argument string does not contain the second argument string.

detaileddescription.para: For example, substring-after("1999/04/01","/") returns 04/01, and substring-after("1999/04/01","19") returns 99/04/01.

briefdescription.para: Implement the normalize-space() XPath function string normalize-space(string?) The normalize-space function returns the argument string with white space normalized by stripping leading and trailing whitespace and replacing sequences of whitespace characters by a single space.

detaileddescription.para: Whitespace characters are the same allowed by the S production in XML. If the argument is omitted, it defaults to the context node converted to a string, in other words the value of the context node.

briefdescription.para: Implement the translate() XPath function string translate(string, string, string) The translate function returns the first argument string with occurrences of characters in the second argument string replaced by the character at the corresponding position in the third argument string.

detaileddescription.para: For example, translate("bar","abc","ABC") returns the string BAr. If there is a character in the second argument string with no character at a corresponding position in the third argument string (because the second argument string is longer than the third argument string), then occurrences of that character in the first argument string are removed. For example, translate("--aaa--","abc-","ABC") returns "AAA". If a character occurs more than once in second argument string, then the first occurrence determines the replacement character. If the third argument string is longer than the second argument string, then excess characters are ignored.

briefdescription.para: Implement the boolean() XPath function boolean boolean(object) The boolean function converts its argument to a boolean as follows:

listitem.para: a number is true if and only if it is neither positive or negative zero nor NaN
a node-set is true if and only if it is non-empty
a string is true if and only if its length is non-zero

briefdescription.para: Implement the not() XPath function boolean not(boolean) The not function returns true if its argument is false, and false otherwise.
Implement the true() XPath function boolean true()
Implement the false() XPath function boolean false()

Implement the lang() XPath function boolean lang(string) The lang function returns true or false depending on whether the language of the context node as specified by xml:lang attributes is the same as or is a sublanguage of the language specified by the argument string.

detaileddescription.para: The language of the context node is determined by the value of the xml:lang attribute on the context node, or, if the context node has no xml:lang attribute, by the value of the xml:lang attribute on the nearest ancestor of the context node that has an xml:lang attribute. If there is no such attribute, then lang returns false. If there is such an attribute, then lang returns true if the attribute value is equal to the argument ignoring case, or if there is some suffix starting with - such that the attribute value is equal to the argument ignoring that suffix of the attribute value and ignoring case.

briefdescription.para: Implement the number() XPath function number number(object?)
Implement the sum() XPath function number sum(node-set) The sum function returns the sum of the values of the nodes in the argument node-set.
Implement the floor() XPath function number floor(number) The floor function returns the largest (closest to positive infinity) number that is not greater than the argument and that is an integer.

Implement the ceiling() XPath function number ceiling(number) The ceiling function returns the smallest (closest to negative infinity) number that is not less than the argument and that is an integer.
Implement the round() XPath function number round(number) The round function returns the number that is closest to the argument and that is an integer.

detaileddescription.para: If there are two such numbers, then the one that is closest to positive infinity is returned.

briefdescription.para: [14] Expr ::= OrExpr [21] OrExpr ::= AndExpr | OrExpr 'or' AndExpr

detaileddescription.para: Parse and compile an expression

parameterdescription.para: whether to sort the resulting node set

briefdescription.para: [8] Predicate ::= '[' PredicateExpr ']' [9] PredicateExpr ::= Expr

detaileddescription.para: Compile a predicate expression

briefdescription.para: [1] LocationPath ::= RelativeLocationPath | AbsoluteLocationPath [2] AbsoluteLocationPath ::= '/' RelativeLocationPath? | AbbreviatedAbsoluteLocationPath [10] AbbreviatedAbsoluteLocationPath ::= '//' RelativeLocationPath

detaileddescription.para: Compile a location path

briefdescription.para: [3] RelativeLocationPath ::= Step | RelativeLocationPath '/' Step | AbbreviatedRelativeLocationPath [11] AbbreviatedRelativeLocationPath ::= RelativeLocationPath '//' Step

detaileddescription.para: Compile a relative location path.

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML non-colonized name.

simplesect.para: the nc name or NULL

briefdescription.para: Parse an XML qualified name.

simplesect.para: the function returns the local part, and prefix is updated to get the Prefix if any.

briefdescription.para: parse an XML name

simplesect.para: the name or NULL

briefdescription.para: [30a] Float ::= Number ('e' Digits?)?

detaileddescription.para: [30] Number ::= Digits ('.' Digits?)? | '.' Digits [31] Digits ::= [0-9]+
Compile a Number in the string In complement of the Number expression, this function also handles negative values : '-' Number.

simplesect.para: the double value.

briefdescription.para: [30] Number ::= Digits ('.

detaileddescription.para: ' Digits?)? | '.' Digits [31] Digits ::= [0-9]+
Compile a Number, then push it on the stack

briefdescription.para: Parse a Literal.

simplesect.para: the value found or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Parse a Literal and push it on the stack.

detaileddescription.para: TODO: Memory allocation could be improved.

briefdescription.para: Parse a VariableReference, evaluate it and push it on the stack.

detaileddescription.para: The variable bindings consist of a mapping from variable names to variable values. The value of a variable is an object, which can be of any of the types that are possible for the value of an expression, and may also be of additional types not specified here.
Early evaluation is possible since: The variable bindings [...] used to evaluate a subexpression are always the same as those used to evaluate the containing expression.

[36] VariableReference ::= '$' QName
ctxt
the XPath Parser context

briefdescription.para: Is the name given a NodeType one.

detaileddescription.para: [38] NodeType ::= 'comment' | 'text' | 'processing-instruction' | 'node'

simplesect.para: 1 if true 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: [16] FunctionCall ::= FunctionName '(' ( Argument ( ',' Argument)*)? ')' [17] Argument ::= Expr

detaileddescription.para: Compile a function call, the evaluation of all arguments are pushed on the stack

briefdescription.para: [15] PrimaryExpr ::= VariableReference | '(' Expr ')' | Literal | Number | FunctionCall

detaileddescription.para: Compile a primary expression.

briefdescription.para: [20] FilterExpr ::= PrimaryExpr | FilterExpr Predicate

detaileddescription.para: Compile a filter expression. Square brackets are used to filter expressions in the same way that they are used in location paths. It is an error if the expression to be filtered does not evaluate to a node-set. The context node list used for evaluating the expression in square brackets is the node-set to be filtered listed in document order.

briefdescription.para: Trickery: parse an XML name but without consuming the input flow Needed to avoid insanity in the parser state.

simplesect.para: the Name parsed or NULL

briefdescription.para: [19] PathExpr ::= LocationPath | FilterExpr | FilterExpr '/' RelativeLocationPath | FilterExpr '//' RelativeLocationPath

detaileddescription.para: Compile a path expression.

briefdescription.para: [18] UnionExpr ::= PathExpr | UnionExpr '|' PathExpr

detaileddescription.para: Compile an union expression.

briefdescription.para: [27] UnaryExpr ::= UnionExpr | '-' UnaryExpr

detaileddescription.para: Compile an unary expression.

briefdescription.para: [26] MultiplicativeExpr ::= UnaryExpr | MultiplicativeExpr MultiplyOperator UnaryExpr | MultiplicativeExpr 'div' UnaryExpr | MultiplicativeExpr 'mod' UnaryExpr [34] MultiplyOperator ::= '*'

detaileddescription.para: Compile an Additive expression.

briefdescription.para: [25] AdditiveExpr ::= MultiplicativeExpr | AdditiveExpr '+' MultiplicativeExpr | AdditiveExpr '-' MultiplicativeExpr

detaileddescription.para: Compile an Additive expression.

briefdescription.para: [24] RelationalExpr ::= AdditiveExpr | RelationalExpr '<' AdditiveExpr | RelationalExpr '>' AdditiveExpr | RelationalExpr '<=' AdditiveExpr | RelationalExpr '>=' AdditiveExpr

detaileddescription.para: A <= B > C is allowed ? Answer from James, yes with (AdditiveExpr <= AdditiveExpr) > AdditiveExpr which is basically what got implemented.
Compile a Relational expression, then push the result on the stack

briefdescription.para: [23] EqualityExpr ::= RelationalExpr | EqualityExpr '=' RelationalExpr | EqualityExpr '!=' RelationalExpr

detaileddescription.para: A != B != C is allowed ? Answer from James, yes with (RelationalExpr = RelationalExpr) = RelationalExpr (RelationalExpr != RelationalExpr) != RelationalExpr which is basically what got implemented.
Compile an Equality expression.

briefdescription.para: [22] AndExpr ::= EqualityExpr | AndExpr 'and' EqualityExpr

detaileddescription.para: Compile an AND expression.

parameterdescription.para: placeholder for a possible name prefix

simplesect.para: the name found and updates test, type and prefix appropriately

briefdescription.para: [6] AxisName ::= 'ancestor' | 'ancestor-or-self' | 'attribute' | 'child' | 'descendant' | 'descendant-or-self' | 'following' | 'following-sibling' | 'namespace' | 'parent' | 'preceding' | 'preceding-sibling' | 'self'

simplesect.para: the axis or 0

briefdescription.para: [4] Step ::= AxisSpecifier NodeTest Predicate* | AbbreviatedStep

detaileddescription.para: [12] AbbreviatedStep ::= '.' | '..'
[5] AxisSpecifier ::= AxisName '::' | AbbreviatedAxisSpecifier
[13] AbbreviatedAxisSpecifier ::= '@'?
Modified for XPtr range support as:
[4xptr] Step ::= AxisSpecifier NodeTest Predicate* | AbbreviatedStep | 'range-to' '(' Expr ')' Predicate*
Compile one step in a Location Path

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the Precompiled XPath operation.

parameterdescription.para: the XPath parser context with the compiled expression

simplesect.para: the number of nodes traversed

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, xmlNodeSetPtr set, int filterOpIndex, int minPos, int maxPos, int hasNsNodes)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathNodeSetFilter

briefdescription.para: Filter a node set, keeping only nodes for which the predicate expression matches.

detaileddescription.para: Afterwards, keep only nodes between minPos and maxPos in the filtered result.

parameterdescription.para: the index of the predicate/filter op
minimum position in the filtered set (1-based)
maximum position in the filtered set (1-based)
true if the node set may contain namespace nodes

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, xmlXPathStepOpPtr op, xmlNodeSetPtr set, int minPos, int maxPos, int hasNsNodes)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathCompOpEvalPredicate

briefdescription.para: Filter a node set, keeping only nodes for which the sequence of predicate expressions matches.

detaileddescription.para: Afterwards, keep only nodes between minPos and maxPos in the filtered result.

parameterdescription.para: minimum position in the filtered set (1-based)
maximum position in the filtered set (1-based)
true if the node set may contain namespace nodes

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathParserContextPtr ctxt, xmlXPathStepOpPtr op, xmlNodePtr *first, xmlNodePtr *last, int toBool)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathNodeCollectAndTest

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the Precompiled XPath operation searching only the last element in document order.

parameterdescription.para: the XPath parser context with the compiled expression
the last elem found so far

simplesect.para: the number of nodes traversed

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the Precompiled XPath expression in the given context.

parameterdescription.para: the XPath parser context with the compiled expression

briefdescription.para: Evaluate a predicate result for the current node.

detaileddescription.para: A PredicateExpr is evaluated by evaluating the Expr and converting the result to a boolean. If the result is a number, the result will be converted to true if the number is equal to the position of the context node in the context node list (as returned by the position function) and will be converted to false otherwise; if the result is not a number, then the result will be converted as if by a call to the boolean function.

simplesect.para: 1 if predicate is true, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Evaluate a predicate result for the current node.

detaileddescription.para: A PredicateExpr is evaluated by evaluating the Expr and converting the result to a boolean. If the result is a number, the result will be converted to true if the number is equal to the position of the context node in the context node list (as returned by the position function) and will be converted to false otherwise; if the result is not a number, then the result will be converted as if by a call to the boolean function.

simplesect.para: 1 if predicate is true, 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Compile an XPath expression.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathCompExpr resulting from the compilation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Compile an XPath expression.

detaileddescription.para: Separate compilation/evaluation entry points.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathCompExpr resulting from the compilation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlXPathCompExprPtr comp, xmlXPathContextPtr ctxt, xmlXPathObjectPtr *resObjPtr, int toBool)
memberdef.name: xmlXPathCompiledEvalInternal

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the Precompiled XPath expression in the given context.

detaileddescription.para: The caller has to free resObj.

parameterdescription.para: the resulting XPath object or NULL
1 if only a boolean result is requested

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the Precompiled XPath expression in the given context.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Applies the XPath boolean() function on the result of the given compiled expression.

simplesect.para: 1 if the expression evaluated to true, 0 if to false and -1 in API and internal errors.

briefdescription.para: Parse and evaluate an XPath expression in the given context, then push the result on the context stack.
Evaluate the XPath Location Path in the given context.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Sets 'node' as the context node.

detaileddescription.para: The node must be in the same document as that associated with the context.

parameterdescription.para: the node to to use as the context node

simplesect.para: -1 in case of error or 0 if successful

briefdescription.para: Evaluate the XPath Location Path in the given context.

detaileddescription.para: The node 'node' is set as the context node. The context node is not restored.

parameterdescription.para: the node to to use as the context node

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Alias for xmlXPathEval.

simplesect.para: the xmlXPathObject resulting from the evaluation or NULL. the caller has to free the object.

briefdescription.para: Registers all default XPath functions in this context.
